texto: The mandarins of culture—what do they do to teach the common folk to read? It's no good writing down lists of books for farmers and compiling five-foot shelves; you've got to go out and visit the people yourself—take the books to them, talk to the teachers and bully the editors of country newspapers and farm magazines and tell the children stories—and then little by little you begin to get good books circulating in the veins of the nation. It's a great work, mind you!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Christopher Moley que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “The mandarins of culture—what do they do to teach the common folk to read? It's no good writing d…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, inspirational-quotes, books, com foco em inspiracao e trabalho.

frase_original: The mandarins of culture—what do they do to teach the common folk to read? It's no good writing down lists of books for farmers and compiling five-foot shelves; you've got to go out and visit the people yourself—take the books to them, talk to the teachers and bully the editors of country newspapers and farm magazines and tell the children stories—and then little by little you begin to get good books circulating in the veins of the nation. It's a great work, mind you!
